summary: The SSRF vulnerability in the Http class allows arbitrary URL fetching without validation. While the PoC was not executed in a live environment, the vulnerability is evident from code analysis showing unrestricted URL acceptance, unlimited redirect following, and lack of private IP filtering.
expected_security_impact: If executed in a cloud environment, the PoC would demonstrate accessing cloud metadata endpoints (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) to retrieve AWS credentials, or scanning internal networks through redirect chains.
actual_observed_result: PoC not attempted. Code analysis confirms the vulnerability: Core/Http.php accepts any URL without protocol validation, enables CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION for unlimited redirects, and lacks DNS rebinding protection. These conditions enable SSRF attacks to internal services and cloud metadata.
verification_method: The vulnerability is verified through static code analysis. Manual testing would require triggering an HTTP request from the application to a controlled URL that redirects to internal endpoints or cloud metadata services.
evidence_produced_by_run: Code analysis shows CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION enabled without limit (Core/Http.php:38)
No CURLOPT_PROTOCOLS restriction to prevent file://, gopher://, dict:// protocols
No validation against private IP ranges or localhost
OpenAI integration passes untrusted URLs from API responses to file_get_contents() (Core/Lib/OpenAi.php:335-336)
limitations_or_blockers: PoC requires triggering an HTTP request from within the application context
Cloud metadata access only possible in AWS/GCP/Azure deployments
Full demonstration requires specific application configuration to expose HTTP client usage

reproduction.source_acquisition: Code analysis only - no live reproduction attempted
